mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS is held high relative to inflation, policy stays restrictive and the 10-year Treasury yield should fall over the next 20 trading days as markets price slower growth and a lower path for future short rates. The signal fails when FEDFUNDS is being cut aggressively or CPIAUCSL is reaccelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are positive in most periods when FEDFUNDS stays elevated and CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, or if the sign flips during sustained easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels signals worsening labor demand and weaker inflation pressure, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when PAYEMS is also soft, confirming broad labor deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if yield changes are not negative after the trigger, or if the trigger is followed by yield increases in more than 60% of cases despite continued PAYEMS weakness.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is above its recent trend, retail demand is holding up and nominal growth expectations rise, which should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if retail sales strength is isolated while labor indicators deteriorate sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend RSAFS is followed by negative 20-day yield changes in a majority of observations, or if the estimated effect is indistinguishable from zero in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO alongside weak HOUST signals broad production and housing contraction, so the 10-year Treasury yield should fall over the next 5 trading days as investors move into duration. The effect fails if industrial weakness is offset by a sharp inflation rebound.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and HOUST does not precede lower 5-day yield changes, or if positive yield moves occur more often than negative moves after the joint signal.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST after already weak GDP data signals a broader housing-driven recession scare, which should push the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if GDP growth rebounds quickly or housing weakness is reversed within one month.
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 60-day yield changes are not negative on average, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for contemporaneous GDP slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is falling while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, real policy restraint tightens and inflation expectations ease, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if inflation stops decelerating or policy is being cut at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if declining CPIAUCSL with flat FEDFUNDS is followed by positive yield changes more often than negative ones, or if the effect disappears once policy easing begins.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS drops while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating quickly and the market should bid down the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days. The signal fails when payroll losses are temporary and unemployment does not confirm the downturn.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls > 100k and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-unemployment shock is not followed by lower 5-day yields, or if the effect is positive in more than half of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, improving INDPRO and rising PAYEMS should lift expected real activity and inflation, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if production improves without labor-market follow-through or if policy tightening overwhelms growth effects.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained gains in INDPRO and PAYEMS are not associated with higher 60-day yields, or if the sign is negative across recovery episodes after the first month.
